steps: Log in to your Zendesk account at https://{your-subdomain}.zendesk.com
Click the grid/waffle icon in the top-right to open Admin Center
In the left sidebar, click 'Apps and integrations'
Ensure 'Token Access' is enabled under the 'Settings' tab
Enter a description for the token (e.g. 'My App Integration')
Copy the generated token — it will not be shown again
Use the token in the Authorization header as: '{email_address}/token:{api_token}' encoded in Base64

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Zendesk API token. Used with email as: Authorization: Basic base64({email}/token:{api_token})

notes: Replace 'example' in the domain with your actual Zendesk subdomain
Requires an Admin account to generate API tokens
Token access must be enabled in Admin Center > Apps and integrations > APIs > Zendesk API > Settings
Token is shown only once — copy and store it immediately
Up to 256 active tokens per account
The base URL is specific to your subdomain: https://{your-subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2
Authentication uses email + token: encode '{email}/token:{api_token}' in Base64 and pass as Basic auth
